discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tape led by semis (SMH +3.78%) and broader tech (XLK +2.76%) with VIX collapsing nearly 7%. Defensive sectors (XLU, XLP, XLRE, XLV) all red, consistent with rotation rather than broad breadth. Standout single-name move is AVAV ripping ~19% on a fresh earnings 8-K following a separate officer-change 8-K days earlier; defense and semi-cap-equipment names dominate the discovery list.

notable_index_moves: SMH +3.78% with TSM +4.94% leading — chip rally extending per Q2 retrospective
Defensives broadly red (XLU -1.5%, XLP -1.5%, XLRE -2.0%, XLV -1.3%) on a green tape — clear rotation out of bond-proxy sectors
VIX -6.8% to 16.45 — vol crush supporting momentum names
ITA +1.4% provides supportive backdrop for defense movers (AVAV, MRCY, KTOS)

discoveries.name: AeroVironment, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Earnings reaction on heavy volume in a defense name with supportive sector tape and prior officer-change 8-K
discoveries.thesis: Sharp move is anchored by a same-day 2.02 earnings 8-K and a recent 5.02 officer change, against a backdrop of defense-budget tailwinds and risk-on tape. Volume ran ~4x average, suggesting institutions are repricing on the print rather than retail chasing. With no obvious overreaction signal (no negative items like 2.05/2.06/4.02 in the filings) and ITA also up, the magnitude looks broadly justified for a small-cap defense name beating into a budget-cycle narrative.
discoveries.what_confirms: Sell-side estimate revisions higher post-print; confirmation that 5.02 was an appointment (e.g., new GM/segment lead) rather than abrupt departure; raised FY guide visible in the 8-K exhibit.
discoveries.what_kills: If the 5.02 turns out to be a CFO/COO unplanned departure, or if the beat was driven by one-time items (e.g., tax benefit) with guide flat/down — then the move is OVERDONE and should fade.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Defense small/mid-caps printing earnings into a confirmed budget-cycle tailwind (ITA green, defense-budget news flow) tend to sustain initial gap moves more than the typical post-earnings drift would suggest — fading on day one without checking guidance is the common mistake.
discoveries.catalyst: Q4 earnings 8-K filed 2026-06-29 (items 2.02, 7.01, 9.01) following a 2026-06-25 8-K with 5.02 officer change item.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Same-day 2.02 results 8-K plus 7.01 FD disclosure (often used to release guidance) is the textbook earnings-beat-with-outlook structure; the 5.02 four days earlier didn't tank the stock, suggesting it was an appointment not a forced departure.
research_pointers: Read the 8-K exhibit 99.1 for actual revenue/EPS vs. consensus and FY guide
Check the 6/25 8-K to determine whether 5.02 was a hire (bullish) or departure (potentially bearish)
Compare AVAV's gap to KTOS (+6.2%) and MRCY (+11%) — sector lift suggests sympathy is contributing ~5-7% of the move
Backlog growth and book-to-bill commentary on conference call

discoveries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
discoveries.setup: Sympathy move in defense peer with no company-specific catalyst and only ~1x volume
discoveries.thesis: MRCY is up double-digits with volume_multiple of only 1.07 and no 8-K, no scheduled earnings — meaning institutions are not heavily participating. The move looks like sympathy with AVAV's print plus the defense-budget narrative. Without conviction-level volume, double-digit sympathy moves in defense names tend to give back a meaningful portion within days as the news-specific tailwind to AVAV doesn't translate cleanly to MRCY's different business mix (embedded computing vs. drones).
discoveries.what_confirms: AVAV gains hold or extend tomorrow while MRCY volume stays light — classic weak-hands sympathy that fades; or a peer note from sell-side explicitly calling out MRCY read-through.
discoveries.what_kills: Disclosure of an actual MRCY-specific catalyst (contract win, M&A rumor) emerges; or volume picks up materially on follow-through day signaling real institutional accumulation.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Sympathy moves in defense peers on another company's earnings are unreliable when volume_multiple stays near 1x — the absence of volume confirmation is the tell that the move is retail/algo-driven and prone to mean-revert.
discoveries.catalyst: No company-specific catalyst detected; likely sympathy with AVAV earnings and broader defense/ITA strength.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals dict and volume only 1.07x average despite ~11% move — pattern of a sympathy move without institutional conviction.
research_pointers: Verify no MRCY-specific news (press release, government contract award database)
Check whether MRCY has been a known short-squeeze candidate (high short interest can amplify sympathy)
Look at MRCY's last earnings reaction — does it typically follow AVAV?
Watch volume on day 2 — if it stays light, fade conviction increases

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap equipment name extending move into a broad semis rally with TSM leading
discoveries.thesis: ONTO up ~8% on the day and ~20% over five days, on a tape where SMH is +3.78% and TSM is +4.94%. Volume_multiple 1.73x suggests real participation. With no 8-K and no scheduled earnings, this looks like a continuation of the chip-rally beta trade tied to advanced packaging exposure — the move is roughly proportional to the sector lift plus a momentum premium. Not an obvious mispricing in either direction.
discoveries.what_confirms: TSM/AVGO commentary on advanced packaging capex; sell-side initiations or upgrades citing HBM exposure.
discoveries.what_kills: Sharp reversal in SMH leads to outsized ONTO drawdown — would suggest beta-leverage was the entire move and the name is over-owned.
discoveries.catalyst: Sector beta — semi rally and advanced packaging narrative; no company-specific signal in 8-K or earnings calendar.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals plus SMH +3.78% and a chip-rally news cycle (CNBC: $2T added to Micron/Intel/AMD in Q2) — move tracks sector beta.
research_pointers: Confirm next earnings date — extended moves into prints are vulnerable
Check short interest and option skew
Compare 5-day move to peers VECO, KLAC, AMAT for relative leadership

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-cap small-cap up 9% on below-average volume with no catalyst signal
discoveries.thesis: VECO gapped ~9% but volume_multiple is only 0.86x — meaning the move happened on light tape. Five-day change only +1.3%, so this is a single-day pop not part of a trend. Sector tailwind (SMH +3.78%) supports some of the lift but doesn't explain the outsized beta. Without an identifiable catalyst and without volume confirmation, I cannot construct a directional thesis at conf 3+.
discoveries.what_confirms: Identification of a specific catalyst — sell-side upgrade, customer contract, M&A chatter — would shift this to UNDERDONE or RATIONAL.
discoveries.what_kills: Volume stays muted and the move retraces within 1-2 sessions — confirms it was thin-tape noise.
discoveries.catalyst: None identified; below-average volume and no 8-K or earnings within window.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals and volume_multiple below 1.0 despite 9% move — atypical pattern that warrants investigation rather than a thesis.
research_pointers: Scan for analyst rating changes today
Check for any pre-announcement or business update press release not yet in news feed
Compare to ONTO — if VECO is lagging the sector trend, this could be catch-up

discoveries.name: Badger Meter, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Water-meter name up 7% on the day and 16% over five days on light volume with no catalyst
discoveries.thesis: BMI isn't a semi or defense name, so the sector-rotation narrative doesn't apply cleanly — it's a scientific instruments / water utilities adjacent business. Volume is below average (0.84x) yet five-day move is +16%, suggesting persistent accumulation without a single news catalyst. Could be a sell-side note, a peer-derived read-through (Xylem, Roper), or M&A speculation — but with no concrete evidence, neither bull nor bear case clears the conf-3 bar.
discoveries.what_confirms: Discovery of a recent sell-side upgrade, water infrastructure spending headline, or M&A rumor would shift to UNDERDONE.
discoveries.what_kills: If the 5-day move is purely momentum/short-covering with no fundamental catalyst, a quick fade is plausible — but I cannot assert that directionally without more data.
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: Empty catalyst_signals, sub-1x volume, but persistent multi-day strength — pattern suggests a known-but-undisclosed-in-this-feed catalyst exists.
research_pointers: Search for recent analyst notes on BMI or water infrastructure
Check XYL, ROP for sympathy linkage
Look at next earnings date — pre-positioning is plausible
Review insider transactions

catalyst_chains.primary_event: AVAV Q4 earnings 8-K with FD disclosure on a supportive defense-budget tape
Continued semi rally with TSM +4.94% and SMH +3.78% — chip-equipment beta lift

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump demanding gasoline retailers cut prices to ~$2.50/gallon, blaming California taxes; references oil at $68
trump_signals.note: Rhetorical pressure on refiners/retailers rather than policy action; XLE is already -0.88% today. Historically these posts produce noise rather than durable moves unless followed by actual executive action. Low conviction signal but worth flagging given XLE underperformance.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as cheaper agentic model
ai_announcements.summary: Sonnet 5 positioned as cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, Gemini Pro with stronger agentic capabilities.
ai_announcements.note: ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL flagged for separate bias grading. No stocks in today's movers list show direct overlap with Sonnet 5's competitive footprint (no visible reaction in software/SaaS names like CRM, NOW, MSFT, GOOGL, or coding-tool incumbents). The movers shown are aerospace/defense and semicap equipment, which are unrelated to this announcement.

ai_announcements.headline: Claude Science workbench for computational research
ai_announcements.summary: Workflow tool for scientists to consolidate databases, pipelines, tools.
ai_announcements.note: ANTHROPIC-RELATED CALL. Potential long-term overlap with scientific software vendors (e.g., Schrodinger SDGR, Certara CERT, Dotmatics, Benchling private). None appear in mover list. No tradable signal today.

ai_announcements.headline: Etched hits $5B valuation, $1B booked sales for inference ASIC
ai_announcements.summary: Nvidia competitor in inference-specific silicon claims $1B contracted revenue.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Inference ASIC competition has been a known overhang for 2+ years (Groq, Cerebras, AWS Trainium, Google TPU). $1B booked is small vs NVDA's ~$130B data center run rate. Etched is single-architecture (transformer-only), brittle to model changes.
research_pointers: NVDA not in mover list — market shrugged
Check if hyperscaler capex commentary references inference ASIC mix

ai_announcements.headline: Nano Banana 2 Lite — cheaper/faster image generation
ai_announcements.summary: Incremental price/speed update to Google's image model.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: Adobe integrates third-party models into Firefly/Creative Cloud; cheaper image gen lowers their inference costs. Workflow lock-in is the moat, not the model.
research_pointers: ADBE not in mover list

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: The actual movers today (AVAV +18.8%, MRCY +11%, KTOS +6.2%, ONTO +7.7%, VECO +9.1%, AEIS +7.1%, BMI +7%) are NOT explained by today's AI news flow. AVAV moved on 8-K earnings (6/29 filing, items 2.02/7.01) — that's an earnings catalyst, not AI. MRCY and KTOS are sympathy moves on defense/drone theme. Semicap names (ONTO, VECO, AEIS) and BMI appear to be sector rotation / capex tape, possibly downstream of broader AI infrastructure enthusiasm but not tied to any specific announcement here. No actionable overdone/underdone AI selloffs in this dataset. Anthropic Sonnet 5 launch is the biggest competitive event but has no visible victim in today's tape — worth monitoring software/SaaS names tomorrow.
